feed.title: Object Perception Lab
feed.subtitle: Dr. Isabel Gauthier's research group at Vanderbilt University studying perceptual expertise, object recognition, and visual cognition.

entry.title: New Study: Object Recognition Ability Predicts AI Face Detection

link.title: New Study: Object Recognition Ability Predicts AI Face Detection

entry.content: A new study from the Object Perception Lab reveals that object recognition ability—not intelligence, AI experience, or face recognition skills—is the strongest predictor of who can identify AI-generated faces. The research, led by Isabel Gauthier with Jason Chow and Rankin McGugin, developed the AI Face Test to measure individual differences in distinguishing real from AI-generated faces.
entry.summary: A new study from the Object Perception Lab reveals that object recognition ability—not intelligence, AI experience, or face recognition skills—is the strongest predictor of who can identify AI-generated faces. The research, led by Isabel Gauthier with Jason Chow and Rankin McGugin, developed the AI Face Test to measure individual differences in distinguishing real from AI-generated faces.

entry.title: Upcoming VSS 2026 Presentations
entry.content:
Upcoming presentations from OPL members and collaborators at VSS 2026:

  • Muller, M., Dobbins, I. & Gauthier, I. Visual Abilities Predict the Content and Quality of Image Descriptions.
  • Chun, T.-Y., Cha, O., Bi, Y., & Gauthier, I. A Common Ensemble Perception Factor for Objects and Faces Strongly Related to General Intelligence.
  • Gauthier, I. Hyperrealism in AI face detection is context-dependent and predicted by object recognition ability.
  • Smithson, C.J., Gronau, N., & Gauthier, I. High-level visual discrimination abilities predict memory for image location as a function of meaningfulness.
entry.summary: Upcoming presentations from OPL members and collaborators at VSS 2026:

entry.title: Annual Review: Is domain-general object recognition ability a novel construct?

link.title: Annual Review: Is domain-general object recognition ability a novel construct?

entry.content: Conor and Isabel’s Annual Review, “Is domain-general object recognition ability a novel construct?” is now available in Annual Review of Psychology.
entry.summary: Conor and Isabel’s Annual Review, “Is domain-general object recognition ability a novel construct?” is now available in Annual Review of Psychology.

entry.title: Congratulations Dr. Smithson!
entry.content: Conor defended his thesis on Nov 5, “The Structure and Correlates of Domain-general Object Recognition Ability”!
entry.summary: Conor defended his thesis on Nov 5, “The Structure and Correlates of Domain-general Object Recognition Ability”!

entry.title: Isabel speaks at the Blue Room

link.title: Isabel speaks at the Blue Room

entry.content: Isabel speaks at the Blue Room (read more at WPLN News).
